scenario: Patient underwent total knee arthroplasty (TKA) on the right knee. Surgeon billed CPT 27447 (TKA) and CPT 27370 (injection of contrast for knee arthrography) on the same day. Patient has ICD-10 M17.11 (primary osteoarthritis, right knee). Conservative care failed after 12 weeks PT and NSAID trial.

billing_flags: CPT 27370 bundled into 27447 per NCCI Column 2 edit — unbundling violation

scenario: Orthopedic surgeon billed CPT 29881 (arthroscopy with meniscectomy) and CPT 29870 (arthroscopy, diagnostic) on the same knee on the same day. ICD-10 M23.201 (derangement of unspecified meniscus, right knee).

billing_flags: CPT 29870 bundled into 29881 — diagnostic arthroscopy included in surgical arthroscopy per NCCI

scenario: Surgeon performed open reduction internal fixation (ORIF) of distal radius fracture CPT 25600 and separately billed CPT 25500 (closed treatment) on the same date. ICD-10 S52.501A (unspecified fracture of lower end of right radius, initial encounter).

billing_flags: CPT 25500 mutually exclusive with 25600 — cannot bill closed and open treatment same fracture same day

scenario: Physical therapist billed CPT 97110 (therapeutic exercise) and CPT 97530 (therapeutic activities) for 60 minutes each on the same day for a single patient with ICD-10 M54.5 (low back pain). Total billed: 4 units each.

billing_flags: MUE exceeded — 97110 and 97530 combined exceed 8 units/day threshold; document medical necessity for each unit

scenario: Surgeon billed CPT 27447 (TKA) with modifier -50 (bilateral procedure) but operative report documents only right knee was operated. ICD-10 M17.11. Facility billed for bilateral implants.

billing_flags: Modifier -50 unsupported — operative report confirms unilateral procedure only; bilateral implant billing inconsistent with single-side documentation

scenario: Oncologist administered Pertuzumab (J9306) and Trastuzumab (J9355) on the same day for HER2+ breast cancer. Also billed CPT 96413 (chemotherapy infusion, first hour) twice. ICD-10 C50.911 (malignant neoplasm of unspecified site of right female breast).

billing_flags: CPT 96413 billed twice — second infusion should be 96417 (each additional hour); first-hour code not repeatable same day

scenario: Radiation oncologist billed CPT 77301 (IMRT planning) and CPT 77295 (3D radiotherapy plan) on the same day for prostate cancer patient. ICD-10 C61 (malignant neoplasm of prostate).

billing_flags: CPT 77295 bundled into 77301 — IMRT planning is more comprehensive and includes 3D planning components

scenario: Oncology nurse billed CPT 96365 (IV infusion, initial) for saline hydration and CPT 96413 (chemotherapy infusion) on the same day. The hydration was given before chemotherapy as pre-medication. ICD-10 C18.9 (colon cancer).

billing_flags: CPT 96365 should be 96361 (additional hour hydration) when given same day as chemotherapy infusion; hydration bundled as sequential service

scenario: Medical oncologist billed J9355 (Trastuzumab) with units coded as 10 (representing 440mg dose). Correct dosing per FDA label for maintenance is 6mg/kg for 75kg patient = 450mg, rounded to nearest vial = 440mg. However claim shows J9355 x 10 units at $1,000/unit. ICD-10 C50.911.

billing_flags: J9355 unit billing requires verification — 10 units at 44mg/unit = 440mg consistent with 75kg maintenance dose; verify actual vial wastage documentation

ncci_citation: CMS Drug Pricing — J9355 per 10mg unit

scenario: Oncologist billed E/M code 99215 (high complexity office visit) on the same day as chemotherapy administration CPT 96413. ICD-10 C50.911. No separate significant evaluation documented beyond the pre-chemo assessment.

billing_flags: 99215 bundled into 96413 on same day — E/M on chemo day requires modifier -25 and documentation of separately identifiable service beyond pre-chemo assessment

ncci_citation: 2026 NCCI Ch. VI §F.1 — E/M with Chemotherapy

scenario: Primary care physician billed 99215 (high complexity E/M) for a patient with Type 2 diabetes (E11.9) presenting for quarterly medication management. Documentation shows 2 chronic conditions reviewed, prescription renewal, and 15-minute visit. MDM documented as moderate.

billing_flags: 99215 requires high MDM or 40+ min total time — documented MDM is moderate (99214 level); potential upcoding by one level

scenario: Hospitalist billed 99233 (subsequent hospital care, high complexity) daily for 5 consecutive days for a stable CHF patient. ICD-10 I50.9. Daily notes are near-identical copy-forward documentation with no new clinical findings.

billing_flags: 99233 requires high MDM — stable patient with copy-forward notes supports 99231 (low complexity) at most; clone documentation is a RAC audit trigger

scenario: Cardiologist billed 99213 (low complexity E/M) and 93000 (ECG with interpretation) on the same day for a patient with atrial fibrillation (I48.91). ECG interpretation note is a single line: 'Atrial fibrillation, rate controlled.'

billing_flags: 93000 global code includes tracing AND interpretation — if interpretation is documented separately it is billable; single-line note may not meet separate interpretation documentation standard

scenario: Psychiatrist billed 99214 (moderate E/M) and 90833 (psychotherapy add-on, 30 min) for a 45-minute combined visit. ICD-10 F32.1 (major depressive disorder, moderate). Documentation supports both medication management and psychotherapy components.

billing_flags: 99214 + 90833 is correct billing for combined med management and psychotherapy visit — no edit triggered when both components documented; verify 45-min total time supports 99214

scenario: Emergency physician billed 99285 (high complexity ED E/M) for a patient presenting with ankle sprain (S93.401A). Documentation shows brief history, limited exam, X-ray ordered, ACE wrap applied, discharge instructions given. Total time 22 minutes.

billing_flags: 99285 requires high MDM or 60+ min — ankle sprain with standard workup supports 99283 (moderate complexity); potential two-level upcoding

scenario: Radiologist billed CPT 71046 (chest X-ray, 2 views) and CPT 71045 (chest X-ray, 1 view) on the same day for the same patient. ICD-10 J18.9 (pneumonia). Report documents PA and lateral views taken together.

billing_flags: CPT 71045 bundled into 71046 — 2-view code includes the single view; unbundling violation

scenario: Radiologist billed CPT 72148 (MRI lumbar spine without contrast) and CPT 72149 (MRI lumbar spine with contrast) separately. ICD-10 M51.16 (intervertebral disc degeneration, lumbar). Should have been billed as CPT 72158 (MRI lumbar spine with and without contrast).

billing_flags: 72148 + 72149 unbundled — combined with/without contrast study must be billed as 72158; separate billing inflates reimbursement

scenario: Hospital billed CPT 74177 (CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast) and CPT 74150 (CT abdomen without contrast) on the same day. ICD-10 R10.9 (unspecified abdominal pain). Radiologist report covers both abdomen and pelvis with contrast only.

billing_flags: CPT 74150 unbundled from 74177 — abdomen-only CT without contrast is a component of the combined abdomen/pelvis with contrast study

scenario: Interventional radiologist billed CPT 36247 (selective catheter placement, third order) and CPT 36245 (first order) and CPT 36246 (second order) for a single catheterization session. ICD-10 I70.219 (atherosclerosis of native arteries of extremities).

billing_flags: 36245 and 36246 bundled into 36247 — only the highest-order selective catheterization is billable per NCCI; lower-order placements are included

scenario: Radiologist billed 26 units of CPT 76942 (ultrasonic guidance for needle placement) in a single month for a single provider. MUE for 76942 is 1 unit per day. ICD-10 M79.3 (panniculitis).

billing_flags: MUE exceeded — 76942 has MUE of 1/day; 26 units in one month across different dates is plausible but requires per-date audit; flag for date-of-service verification

scenario: Cardiologist billed CPT 93000 (ECG global — tracing, interpretation, report) and CPT 93010 (ECG interpretation only) on the same day for the same patient. ICD-10 I25.10 (atherosclerotic heart disease).

billing_flags: CPT 93010 is a component of 93000 global — cannot bill both; 93010 bundled into 93000

scenario: Cardiologist billed CPT 93306 (echocardiogram with Doppler) and CPT 93320 (Doppler echocardiography) separately. ICD-10 I34.0 (mitral valve regurgitation). Both performed in same session.

billing_flags: CPT 93320 Doppler component bundled into 93306 — complete echo with Doppler includes the Doppler study; separate billing is unbundling

scenario: Cardiologist performed cardiac catheterization CPT 93458 (left heart cath) and billed CPT 93454 (coronary angiography) separately. ICD-10 I25.110 (atherosclerotic heart disease with unstable angina). Both performed in same session.

billing_flags: CPT 93454 bundled into 93458 — left heart cath with coronary angiography is a single comprehensive code; 93454 is a component

scenario: Electrophysiologist billed CPT 93619 (EP study, comprehensive) and CPT 93600 (His bundle recording) on the same day. ICD-10 I49.9 (cardiac arrhythmia). Both documented in same procedure note.

billing_flags: CPT 93600 bundled into 93619 — comprehensive EP study includes His bundle recording as a component

scenario: Cardiologist billed CPT 93017 (exercise stress test, tracing only) and CPT 93016 (exercise stress test, physician supervision only) separately. ICD-10 I20.9 (unstable angina). Physician supervised and interpreted the test.

billing_flags: 93016 + 93017 should be billed as 93015 (global stress test) when same physician supervises and interprets — split billing of global components is an unbundling violation

ncci_citation: 2026 NCCI Ch. V §B.2 — Stress Testing Global/Component

scenario: Anesthesiologist billed CPT 00402 (anesthesia for reconstructive breast surgery) for a bilateral mastectomy with immediate reconstruction. ICD-10 C50.911. Also billed CPT 00400 (anesthesia for superficial procedures on integumentary system) for the same case.

billing_flags: CPT 00400 mutually exclusive with 00402 — site-specific anesthesia code 00402 supersedes general integumentary code 00400 for the same surgical field

ncci_citation: 2026 NCCI Ch. II §B — Anesthesia Mutual Exclusivity

scenario: CRNA billed 18 base units for CPT 00630 (anesthesia for lumbar spine surgery) plus 12 time units (60 minutes at 1 unit/15 min). Surgeon's operative note documents 45-minute procedure. ICD-10 M51.16.

billing_flags: Time unit discrepancy — 12 time units implies 60 minutes but surgeon documents 45 minutes (9 time units); 3 excess time units billed; verify anesthesia start/stop times

ncci_citation: CMS Claims Processing Manual Ch. 12 §50 — Anesthesia Time Units

scenario: Anesthesiologist billed CPT 01967 (neuraxial labor analgesia) and CPT 01968 (cesarean delivery add-on) for a patient who had epidural placed for labor that converted to C-section. ICD-10 O82 (encounter for cesarean delivery). Modifier -QZ used.

billing_flags: 01967 + 01968 is correct billing for labor epidural converting to C-section — this is a valid add-on code combination; no NCCI edit triggered; verify QZ modifier appropriate for CRNA without medical direction

scenario: Anesthesiologist billed separately for CPT 99100 (anesthesia qualifying circumstance — extreme age, patient is 3 years old) in addition to the base anesthesia code. ICD-10 Q21.1 (atrial septal defect). Pediatric cardiac surgery.

billing_flags: CPT 99100 is a valid qualifying circumstance add-on for patients under 1 year or over 70 — patient age 3 does not qualify for 99100; extreme age qualifier applies to under 1 year only per CPT guidelines

scenario: Anesthesiologist billed CPT 01830 (anesthesia for wrist/hand surgery) for a carpal tunnel release. ICD-10 G56.01. Also billed CPT 64721 (neuroplasty, median nerve at carpal tunnel) — the surgeon's code — on the same claim.

billing_flags: CPT 64721 is the surgeon's procedure code — anesthesiologist should not bill surgical procedure codes; 64721 on anesthesia claim is a cross-billing error

ncci_citation: CMS Claims Processing Manual Ch. 12 §40 — Provider Billing Scope

scenario: Urologist billed CPT 52000 (cystoscopy) and CPT 52204 (cystoscopy with biopsy) on the same day for the same patient. ICD-10 N30.00 (acute cystitis without hematuria). Single procedure session documented.

billing_flags: CPT 52000 bundled into 52204 — diagnostic cystoscopy is included in cystoscopy with biopsy; billing both is an unbundling violation per NCCI Ch. VII §2

scenario: Urologist billed CPT 55700 (prostate biopsy, needle) 12 times (12 cores) as 12 separate line items. ICD-10 N40.1 (enlarged prostate with LUTS). MUE for 55700 is 1 unit per day.

billing_flags: MUE violation — 55700 has MUE of 1/day regardless of number of cores; multi-core biopsy is a single procedure; 11 excess units billed

scenario: Urologist billed CPT 52310 (cystoscopy with removal of foreign body) and CPT 52315 (cystoscopy with removal of calculus) on the same day. ICD-10 N21.0 (calculus in bladder). Single operative session.

billing_flags: CPT 52310 and 52315 are mutually exclusive for same-session cystoscopy — 52315 (calculus removal) is the appropriate code; 52310 is a lower-complexity version for foreign body

scenario: Urologist billed CPT 52647 (laser vaporization of prostate) and CPT 52601 (TURP) on the same day for the same patient. ICD-10 N40.1. Operative report documents laser vaporization only.

billing_flags: CPT 52601 mutually exclusive with 52647 — cannot bill TURP and laser vaporization for same prostate surgery; operative report supports only 52647

scenario: Urologist billed CPT 50590 (lithotripsy, extracorporeal shock wave) and CPT 52353 (ureteroscopy with lithotripsy) on the same day for a 6mm ureteral stone. ICD-10 N20.1 (calculus of ureter). Two separate procedure notes exist.

billing_flags: CPT 50590 and 52353 are mutually exclusive for same stone same day — two different lithotripsy approaches for the same calculus cannot be billed together; ureteroscopic approach (52353) is the definitive procedure

scenario: Home health agency coded OASIS-E item M1800 (grooming) as 1 (requires assistance) when nursing notes consistently document patient performs grooming independently. ICD-10 I69.351 (hemiplegia following cerebral infarction). PDGM episode billed at higher functional impairment level.

billing_flags: OASIS M1800 upcoding — nursing notes contradict OASIS score; grooming coded as dependent when documented as independent; PDGM case-mix inflated; OIG Home Health Work Plan 2026 priority

ncci_citation: CMS OASIS-E Guidance Manual 2026 — M1800 Scoring

scenario: Home health agency billed for 60-day PDGM episode with 5 skilled nursing visits and 3 PT visits. OASIS-E discharge assessment documents patient achieved all therapy goals by day 21. Agency did not discharge until day 60. ICD-10 M17.11.

billing_flags: Extended episode despite goal achievement — failure to discharge when goals met inflates PDGM episode payment; CMS expects discharge or recertification when clinical goals are achieved; potential false claims exposure

ncci_citation: CMS PDGM Technical Report 2026 — Episode Timing

scenario: Home health agency coded primary diagnosis as Z96.641 (presence of right artificial knee joint) instead of the active clinical condition M17.11 (osteoarthritis) driving the home health need. PDGM clinical grouping changes significantly based on primary diagnosis.

billing_flags: Primary diagnosis miscoding — Z96.641 is a status code, not a clinical condition; active condition driving home health need must be primary; PDGM clinical group assignment affected; potential upcoding

ncci_citation: CMS PDGM ICD-10 Coding Guidelines 2026 — Primary Diagnosis Selection

scenario: Home health agency billed G0299 (skilled nursing visit) for a visit documented only as 'medication reminder' with no clinical assessment, wound care, or skilled intervention. ICD-10 E11.9 (Type 2 diabetes). Patient is otherwise independent.

billing_flags: Skilled nursing visit not medically necessary — medication reminder alone does not meet CMS skilled care criteria; visit should be downgraded to non-covered or home health aide level; potential false claim

ncci_citation: CMS Medicare Benefit Policy Manual Ch. 7 §40.1 — Skilled Nursing Criteria

scenario: Home health agency billed for PT evaluation CPT equivalent G0151 on day 1 and again on day 3 of the same PDGM episode. ICD-10 S72.001A (femoral neck fracture). Two separate PT evaluation notes exist but cover the same functional baseline.

billing_flags: Duplicate PT evaluation billing — two evaluations within 3 days of same episode without documented change in condition or new referral; second evaluation likely not separately billable

ncci_citation: CMS Medicare Benefit Policy Manual Ch. 7 §40.2 — Therapy Evaluation Frequency

scenario: DME supplier billed HCPCS K0001 (standard manual wheelchair) and separately billed E0950 (wheelchair footrests) and E0951 (wheelchair armrests) as individual line items. ICD-10 G35 (multiple sclerosis). Footrests and armrests are standard wheelchair components.

billing_flags: E0950 and E0951 are standard wheelchair accessories bundled into K0001 — cannot bill standard components separately; unbundling violation per CMS DME MAC policy

ncci_citation: CMS DME MAC Policy Article — Wheelchair Accessories Bundling

scenario: DME supplier billed K0835 (power wheelchair, Group 3, standard) for a patient with ICD-10 G12.21 (ALS). Also billed E2310 (power wheelchair joystick) and E2311 (power wheelchair controller) as separate line items. These are standard components of K0835.

billing_flags: E2310 and E2311 are standard components of K0835 power wheelchair — separate billing is unbundling; only upgrades or non-standard accessories are separately billable

ncci_citation: CMS DME MAC LCD L33702 — Power Mobility Devices

scenario: DME supplier billed A6216 (gauze, non-impregnated, sterile, pad size 16 sq in or less) with quantity 200 for a single 30-day supply for a diabetic foot ulcer patient. ICD-10 E11.621. CMS MUE for A6216 is 100 units per day.

billing_flags: MUE exceeded — A6216 quantity 200 for 30-day supply averages 6.7/day which is within daily MUE but total quantity requires medical necessity documentation; verify wound size and dressing change frequency in clinical notes

scenario: DME supplier billed E0601 (CPAP device) and separately billed A7030 (full face mask), A7031 (face mask interface), A7032 (cushion for full face mask), and A7034 (nasal interface) all on the same claim. ICD-10 G47.33 (obstructive sleep apnea). Initial setup.

billing_flags: A7031 and A7034 cannot be billed with A7030 on same claim — full face mask (A7030) and nasal interface (A7034) are mutually exclusive mask types; A7031 is a component of A7030; bill only one mask type per setup

ncci_citation: CMS DME MAC Policy — CPAP Accessories

scenario: DME supplier billed L3000 (foot insert, removable, molded to patient) and L3010 (foot insert, removable, longitudinal arch) for the same foot on the same date. ICD-10 M21.6X1 (acquired deformity of right foot). Both are custom orthotics.

billing_flags: L3000 and L3010 are mutually exclusive for same foot same date — only one custom foot orthotic insert is billable per foot per date; L3010 is a less comprehensive version of L3000

ncci_citation: CMS DME MAC LCD L33369 — Orthotics

scenario: Psychiatrist billed CPT 90837 (psychotherapy, 60 min) as a standalone code without an E/M code. ICD-10 F33.1 (major depressive disorder, recurrent, moderate). Visit documentation shows medication management discussion and psychotherapy. Duration 55 minutes.

billing_flags: 90837 billed standalone — when medication management is also performed, correct billing is E/M code + psychotherapy add-on (90833/90836/90838); 55 minutes supports 99214 + 90836 (45 min add-on)

scenario: Psychologist billed CPT 90834 (psychotherapy, 45 min) and CPT 90785 (interactive complexity add-on) for a standard adult therapy session. ICD-10 F41.1 (generalized anxiety disorder). No documentation of interactive complexity criteria met.

billing_flags: CPT 90785 requires documentation of at least one interactive complexity criterion (e.g., third-party involvement, play equipment, complex communication) — standard adult therapy without documented criterion does not support 90785

scenario: Licensed clinical social worker billed CPT 99213 (E/M, low complexity) and CPT 90837 (psychotherapy, 60 min) on the same claim. ICD-10 F32.0 (major depressive disorder, mild). LCSWs cannot bill E/M codes under Medicare.

billing_flags: E/M code 99213 not billable by LCSW under Medicare — LCSWs are limited to psychotherapy codes (90832-90838) and cannot bill E/M codes; 99213 must be removed from claim

ncci_citation: CMS Medicare Benefit Policy Manual Ch. 15 §150 — LCSW Coverage

scenario: Psychiatrist billed CPT 90838 (psychotherapy add-on, 60 min) with CPT 99212 (low complexity E/M, 10-19 min). ICD-10 F31.81 (bipolar II disorder). Total visit time documented as 75 minutes.

billing_flags: 99212 + 90838 is internally inconsistent — 90838 (60-min add-on) requires a substantial E/M; 99212 (10-19 min) is too brief to support a 60-min psychotherapy add-on; total 75 min supports 99215 + 90838

scenario: Group practice billed CPT 90853 (group psychotherapy) for 12 patients in a single session and also billed CPT 90837 (individual psychotherapy, 60 min) for 3 of those same patients for the same date. ICD-10 F10.20 (alcohol use disorder, moderate).

billing_flags: CPT 90837 cannot be billed same date as 90853 for same patients — individual and group therapy are mutually exclusive on the same date per CMS; the 3 patients billed for both represent duplicate billing

ncci_citation: CMS Medicare Claims Processing Manual Ch. 9 §60 — Group vs Individual Therapy
